title: Tens of Thousands March in Kolhapur Against ₹86,300 Crore Shaktipeeth Expressway
summary: Farmers from 12 districts across Maharashtra held a massive 'Mahamorcha' in Kolhapur on June 9 against the proposed Nagpur-Goa Shaktipeeth Expressway, demanding its cancellation over fears of large-scale fertile farmland acquisition. Women farmers asked: what use are temples along the route if the land feeding them is destroyed?

coverages.headline: Farmers take out 'Mahamorcha' in Kolhapur to protest proposed Shaktipeeth Expressway
coverages.excerpt: Tens of thousands of farmers from 12 districts marched in Kolhapur against the ₹86,300-crore Nagpur-Goa Shaktipeeth Expressway, demanding its cancellation over mass farmland acquisition fears.
coverages.bias_notes: Wire-service framing is largely neutral — reports the protest's scale and demands without editorialising. What's missing: the government's position on land acquisition compensation, any farmer voices who support the development, or how the Shaktipeeth project compares to other Maharashtra expressway controversies in terms of the land-per-km footprint.

coverages.headline: Maharashtra farmers protest against Rs 86,300 crore Shaktipeeth Highway project
coverages.excerpt: Farmer women held protests questioning the utility of temples along the route if fertile land is destroyed — images from the march went viral on social media.
coverages.bias_notes: National Herald is Congress-affiliated, so its coverage of a farmers' protest against a BJP-led government project carries an inherent sympathetic lean. The viral image angle is real and newsworthy but the article does not include any BJP or Fadnavis government response, presenting only one side of the dispute.
